message:
feat(runtime_compat): add three-branch select! arms + refactor to shared @poll arms

Extends the `select!` macro to natively support three-branch
selection while keeping the existing two-branch arms source-compatible.

Implementation pattern: two private helper arms

  select!(@poll2 (<p1,f1,b1>) (<p2,f2,b2>))
  select!(@poll3 (<p1,f1,b1>) (<p2,f2,b2>) (<p3,f3,b3>))

hold the actual `futures::future::select` + `pin_mut!` + `Either`
scaffolding. Every public arm forwards to one of these helpers so the
frame-logic lives in exactly one place:

  biased;                        -> @poll2
  two branches (block bodies)    -> @poll2
  two branches (block + expr)    -> @poll2
  two branches (expr bodies)     -> @poll2
  three branches (block bodies)  -> @poll3
  three branches (expr  bodies)  -> @poll3

For three branches the implementation stacks
`futures::future::select(fut2, fut3)` then wraps that with
`futures::future::select(fut1, ...)`, dispatching on the nested
`Either::Left / Either::Right` layers to route the appropriate body.
This keeps the behavior left-biased on fut1 and then fut2 without
pulling in a dependency on tokio's `select!`.

Also adds two previously missing call-shape arms:

- Three-branch, block bodies (no trailing comma required)
- Two-branch with mixed block + expression bodies

The macro is still `#[macro_export]`-annotated and re-exported via
`pub use crate::select` so downstream crates and
`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` keep working unchanged. No existing
call sites become ambiguous; `cargo expand` output for the two-branch
cases is byte-equivalent to the prior inline implementation (same
Arc of operations: pin, select, match Left/Right).
